entries.description: Read spec.md for set 005. Identified Session 1 deliverables: ai-router/queue_status.py + ai-router/heartbeat_status.py Python helpers (invokable as python -m ai_router.queue_status / heartbeat_status), package.json view-container wiring for Provider Queues and Provider Heartbeats, and unit tests. User instruction: no external routing this session except cross-provider verification, which goes to Gemini Pro.
Registered session-state.json for session 1 with orchestrator metadata (claude-opus-4-7, high effort).

Created ai-router/queue_status.py: collect_status() walks <base_dir>/<provider>/queue.db and emits the schema {providers: {<name>: {queue_path, queue_present, states, messages}}}. Reads via QueueDB short-lived connections so daemon writers are not starved. Includes get_payload() for the extension's Open Payload action and two emergency-intervention paths: mark_failed() forces state='failed' regardless of who claims (operator escape hatch for stuck claims with live leases), force_reclaim() releases a stuck claim back to state='new' and bumps attempts. Both interventions use BEGIN IMMEDIATE / COMMIT / ROLLBACK directly rather than the QueueDB ownership-checked methods. CLI: --workspace, --base-dir, --provider, --state, --limit, --format text|json, --mark-failed, --force-reclaim, --get-payload.

Created ai-router/heartbeat_status.py. collect_status() reads capacity_signal.jsonl via the existing read_capacity_summary() and emits per-provider {signal_path, signal_file_present, last_completion_at, minutes_since_last_completion, completions_in_last_<N>min, tokens_in_last_<N>min, lookback_minutes, _disclaimer}. The DISCLAIMER constant is duplicated at both top level and per-provider so a consumer that renders only one provider still sees it. Field names embed the lookback value so the extension's tree label can read e.g. completions_in_last_60min directly. CLI: --workspace, --base-dir, --provider, --lookback-minutes, --format text|json.

Wired tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/package.json: added view containers dabblerProviderQueues and dabblerProviderHeartbeats under the existing dabblerSessionSetsContainer; registered command IDs (refresh, openPayload, markFailed, forceReclaim) as placeholders for Sessions 2 and 3; added view/title and view/item/context menu entries; added six configuration settings (autoRefreshSeconds, pythonPath, messageLimit for queues; autoRefreshSeconds, lookbackMinutes, silentWarningMinutes for heartbeats). Bumped extension version to 0.10.0 and added a CHANGELOG entry.

Wrote ai-router/tests/test_queue_status.py (23 tests) and tests/test_heartbeat_status.py (11 tests). Coverage: collect_status shape, provider discovery vs filtering, state-filter and limit honoring, get_payload, mark_failed/force_reclaim happy paths and refusals (terminal state, non-claimed state, unknown id), CLI smoke tests via subprocess, disclaimer placement, lookback-embedded field names, mutually-exclusive-flag CLI rejection. All 34 new tests pass; full ai-router suite (591 tests) passes with no regressions.

Cross-provider verification routed to gemini-pro per session instruction. Result: ISSUES_FOUND. Substantive findings: (1 Major) force_reclaim incorrectly set failure_reason='manual_force_reclaim_via_queue_status' on a state='new' transition, conflating retry with failure; (2 Major) heartbeat_status.collect_status had a redundant if/else where both branches did providers = [provider_filter]; (3 Suggestion) _print_text in heartbeat_status reconstructed embedded-N field names from the function argument rather than the payload itself, creating fragile coupling. False-positive: 'completed_at field violates schema' (the docstring lists representative fields, not an exhaustive enum; completed_at is useful for terminal-state messages). Non-substantive: argparse mutually_exclusive_group preference (working code, style only); convoluted provider_filter shape (functional). See session-reviews/session-001.md for full review.

Fixes: (1) force_reclaim now sets failure_reason = NULL on the state='new' transition and a comment explains why operator audit trail lives in command history, not in the row; (2) heartbeat_status collapsed the redundant if/else to a single providers = [provider_filter] assignment; (3) _print_text in heartbeat_status now derives n from info['lookback_minutes'] inside the loop, falling back to the function argument only as a default. All 34 module tests still pass after fixes.

Bumped session-state.json currentSession from 1 to 2 and reread spec for Session 2 deliverables (ProviderQueuesProvider TS module, refresh + auto-poll, right-click context menu commands for Open Payload, Mark Failed, Force Reclaim, tests).

Added src/utils/pythonRunner.ts: spawn helper for python -m <module>. Resolves the configured python executable via dabblerProviderQueues.pythonPath setting; relative paths are workspace-relative so a checked-in .venv works without machine-specific config. Always resolves with {stdout, stderr, exitCode, signal, timedOut}; never throws so the tree-view caller can render an error info node without try/catch noise. 10s default timeoutMs.

Added src/providers/ProviderQueuesProvider.ts implementing vscode.TreeDataProvider. Tree shape: root -> providers (alphabetic) -> state buckets (lifecycle order: new, claimed, completed, failed, timed_out) -> message nodes. Spec risk-mitigation: 5s result cache (CACHE_TTL_MS) so a tree expand/collapse cycle doesn't re-spawn Python; concurrent getChildren() calls fold onto a single in-flight promise. State-bucket message lists are filtered from the per-provider message slice; if the count exceeds the slice size (Python --limit), an info node 'N more not shown' is appended so the operator never assumes the queue is shorter than reality. Codicons by state (circle-large-outline, sync, pass, error, watch). Tooltip on each message includes id, task_type, session_set, attempts, claimed_by, lease_expires_at, completed_at. Single-click on a message routes to dabblerProviderQueues.openPayload (same as right-click). parseFetchResult helper exported for testing.

Added src/commands/queueActions.ts with three commands and a content provider. Open Payload: shells out to queue_status --get-payload, renders the JSON into a dabbler-queue-payload:// virtual document (read-only by construction; payloads must not be mutated mid-flight). Mark Failed: modal confirmation dialog + queue_status --mark-failed; refuses non-existent or terminal messages via the Python helper's existing checks. Force Reclaim: modal confirmation + queue_status --force-reclaim; refuses non-claimed messages. Both interventions surface success/failure via VS Code information/error messages and refresh the tree.

Wired src/extension.ts: registered ProviderQueuesProvider against the dabblerProviderQueues view, registered the dabblerProviderQueues.refresh command, set up a configurable auto-poll (dabblerProviderQueues.autoRefreshSeconds, default 15s; 0 disables; rebinds on settings change). Registered the three queue-action commands via registerQueueActionCommands.

Wrote src/test/suite/providerQueues.test.ts covering: tree shape contract (alphabetic provider order, absent-queue info node, lifecycle-ordered state buckets, message-state isolation, 'more not shown' overflow node, empty-payload guidance, fetch-failure error node), tree-item rendering (description totals, openPayload command on message items, expand/collapse heuristic for completed vs claimed buckets), and parseFetchResult JSON parser (valid, timeout, non-zero exit, malformed JSON, missing providers field). 14 new tests written.

TypeScript type-check (npx tsc --noEmit) clean. esbuild bundle (npm run compile) clean. vsce ls validates the manifest. The Electron-based test harness (@vscode/test-electron) is blocked by a pre-existing Windows launcher problem unrelated to this session: Code.exe rejects the test runner's --no-sandbox / --user-data-dir flags. Same harness was non-functional during Set 4. Tracked as a follow-up; tests are written against the same suite()/test() shape as the existing metrics/fileSystem tests and will run once the harness is unstuck.

Cross-provider verification routed to gemini-pro per session instruction. Result: VERIFIED. Verifier flagged correctness (tree view, data fetch, caching, all spec'd context menu actions including confirmation dialogs and view refreshing; robust error handling on the subprocess), completeness (all spec deliverables present; tests cover key logic paths), and code quality (notable: in-flight-promise dedupe in _getPayload and configurable settings). No issues to address. See session-reviews/session-002.md.

Bumped session-state.json currentSession from 2 to 3 (pending). Reread spec.md for Session 3 deliverables: ProviderHeartbeatsProvider tree view with explicit observational framing, mode badges ([FIRST]/[LAST]) on existing session-set tree items via outsourceMode parsing of spec.md, tests, README updates. User pin reaffirmed: route only the cross-provider verification, target Gemini Pro.

Added OutsourceMode = 'first' | 'last' to src/types.ts and threaded outsourceMode: OutsourceMode through SessionSetConfig (default 'first' for backward compat). Extended parseSessionSetConfig in src/utils/fileSystem.ts to read the field from the same yaml block it already scans, accepting only 'first' or 'last' (anything else falls back to 'first').

Created src/providers/ProviderHeartbeatsProvider.ts implementing vscode.TreeDataProvider<HeartbeatTreeNode>. Tree shape: root -> per-provider leaf nodes (no further expansion; each row carries its own description and tooltip). Shells out to python -m ai_router.heartbeat_status --format json via the existing pythonRunner; reuses dabblerProviderQueues.pythonPath setting so users only configure Python once. 5s result cache (CACHE_TTL_MS) keyed on (root, lookback) so a lookback change invalidates immediately; concurrent getChildren calls fold onto a single in-flight promise. parseFetchResult normalizes the helper's embedded-N field names (completions_in_last_60min) into stable shape via normalizeProvider; defensively falls back to the requested lookback if the payload disagrees. isSilent treats no signal file or never-completed-yet as silent (operator can't distinguish 'never ran' from 'stopped'). formatMinutesAgo: null -> 'never', <60 -> 'Nm ago', >=60 -> 'Hh Mm ago'. Per-row icon: pulse when active, warning when silent. Exported HEARTBEAT_FOOTER constant for the view-description footer.

Wired ProviderHeartbeatsProvider into src/extension.ts via createTreeView (rather than registerTreeDataProvider) so we can set treeView.description = HEARTBEAT_FOOTER — keeps the observational-only disclaimer visible at the view header at all times. Registered dabblerProviderHeartbeats.refresh command and a configurable auto-poll (dabblerProviderHeartbeats.autoRefreshSeconds, default 15s; 0 disables; rebinds on settings change). Also rebinds + refreshes when lookbackMinutes or silentWarningMinutes change.

Added modeBadge() helper to src/providers/SessionSetsProvider.ts: returns '[FIRST]' or '[LAST]' from set.config.outsourceMode. Inserted into the bits.join chain on each session-set row's description. Added 'Mode: outsource-<mode>' line to the row tooltip's config block. Backward-compat: when a spec omits outsourceMode, parseSessionSetConfig fills in 'first', so [FIRST] renders on existing repos with no spec changes.

Added two test files. src/test/suite/providerHeartbeats.test.ts (19 tests): tree shape (alphabetic provider order, leaves only, empty payload guidance, fetch failure, missing workspace), silent-warning threshold (active <30m, silent at 31m, exact-30m boundary not-silent, no signal file silent, no completions silent, icon + contextValue assertions), rendering (description format, missing-signal text, tooltip echoes disclaimer, formatMinutesAgo edge cases including 0/45/120/202), parseFetchResult (embedded-N normalization, lookback mismatch fallback, timeout/non-zero/malformed/missing-providers rejection). src/test/suite/modeBadge.test.ts (7 tests): outsourceMode parsing (default-when-missing, default-when-omitted-in-yaml, last, first explicit, unknown-falls-back-to-first) and modeBadge rendering for both modes.

Updated tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/README.md: added 'Provider Queues' and 'Provider Heartbeats' feature sections (the latter prominently flagging the observational-only framing in a blockquote), an 'Outsource modes' subsection explaining first vs. last semantics, a 'mode badges' note under Session Set Explorer, six new rows in the Extension Settings table (autoRefreshSeconds + pythonPath + messageLimit for queues; autoRefreshSeconds + lookbackMinutes + silentWarningMinutes for heartbeats), and outsourceMode in the example Session Set Configuration block.

Bumped extension version 0.10.0 -> 0.11.0; added a 0.11.0 CHANGELOG entry covering Provider Heartbeats view, mode badges, and auto-refresh wiring. TypeScript type-check (npx tsc --noEmit) clean. esbuild bundle (npm run compile) clean. vsce ls validates the manifest. vsce package builds dabbler-ai-orchestration-0.11.0.vsix (17 files, 289.92 KB). Electron test harness still blocked by the same pre-existing Windows-launcher problem flagged in Sessions 1 and 2; not introduced by this session.

Cross-provider verification routed to gemini-pro per session instruction (input 12,557; output 276; cost $0.0185). Result: one suggestion-level finding, no defects. Verifier observed that the dabblerProviderHeartbeats config-change listener calls rebindHeartbeatsPoll() unconditionally for any of the three settings even though only autoRefreshSeconds drives the setInterval; the other two only need a refresh. Applied the suggested split in src/extension.ts: affectsTiming gates rebindHeartbeatsPoll, affectsTiming || affectsContent gates the refresh. Recompiled clean and re-packaged 0.11.0 VSIX.
